tooling_attempts.reason: Claude CLI reported no repository file-system tools and requested pasted diffs; no code verdict was produced.

findings.title: Contract pre-declares production_ready while S07 closeout evidence is absent and rp00:control-plane:validate fails
findings.evidence: contracts/control-plane-contract.json flips current_subphase to RP00.P01.M05.S07 with status "production_ready" and adds the S07 subphase_closeouts entry as "production_ready" (evidence_root docs/goal-closeout/rp00-p01-m05-s07). The completion_gates now point claude_cross_validation/production_readiness at docs/goal-closeout/rp00-p01-m05-s07, and the validator adds a construction-inspection check requiring inspection.human_approval_reference_relationship_map_verified === true for that closeout. The supplied run shows `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate` exit_code 1 (expected_pending: S07 evidence files not created yet), so no Claude C00 result, Hermes H00 row, or construction-inspection.json exists under that evidence_root. The diff alone cannot satisfy hermes_validation, claude_cross_validation, construction_inspection, or production_readiness gates.
findings.recommendation: Before certifying production_ready, record the actual claude-opus-4-8 effort=max read-only C00 review result (claude-review-result.json), the Hermes H00 evidence row, and construction-inspection.json with human_approval_reference_relationship_map_verified=true under docs/goal-closeout/rp00-p01-m05-s07, then re-run `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate` and confirm exit 0. This is the expected final closeout step; the code, fixture, contract definition, and tests are otherwise complete.

findings.title: Divergent blocked_claim reference patterns between S06 ownership routes and S07 relationship map
findings.evidence: packages/control-plane/src/model.js keeps HUMAN_APPROVAL_CORRECTION_ROUTE_PATTERN from S06 with underscore-only segments ((?:_[a-z0-9]+)*) while S07 adds HUMAN_APPROVAL_BLOCKED_CLAIM_REF_PATTERN allowing dot-or-underscore segments ((?:[._][a-z0-9]+)*). The same conceptual blocked_claim.* reference is therefore accepted under two different canonical shapes across HumanApproval slices.
findings.recommendation: Unify the blocked_claim reference pattern across HumanApproval slices, or explicitly document why relationship-map blocked_claim refs may contain dotted segments while ownership correction routes may not.

findings.title: Mixed tabs and spaces introduced in the contract validator
findings.evidence: scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs introduces tab-indented lines in several added/edited regions (the closing braces after the S06 ownership block, the construction-inspection check adding human_approval_reference_relationship_map_verified, the text-presence arrays around RP00.P01.M05.S05/S06/S07, and the HUMAN_APPROVAL_REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIP_* export list), whereas the rest of the file uses space indentation. node --check passes (exit_code 0), so this is cosmetic only, but it breaks indentation consistency.
findings.recommendation: Normalize the new lines to space indentation and run the repository formatter/linter so the validator stays internally consistent.

findings.title: Optional reference arrays must be present as keys despite relationshipMap marking them required:false
findings.evidence: HUMAN_APPROVAL_REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIP_POLICY.relationshipMap marks blocked_claim_refs, hermes_gate_refs, and claude_review_gate_refs as required:false with cardinality zero_or_many, but validateHumanApprovalReferenceRelationships calls assertControlPlaneStringArray(value.<field>, ..., { allowEmpty: true }) unconditionally for each. Omitting one of these keys would throw rather than be treated as zero references; the 'empty optional' coverage in model.test.js and the validator both pass explicit [] values, never omitted keys.
findings.recommendation: Decide the intended contract: if omitted keys should count as zero references, coerce undefined to [] before assertControlPlaneStringArray; otherwise document that the relationship map requires present-but-possibly-empty arrays. Formal required/optional field presence is owned by RP00.P01.M05.S08 (Required field registry), so reconcile there.

findings.title: Contract pre-declares production_ready while S07 closeout evidence is absent and rp00:control-plane:validate fails
findings.details: contracts/control-plane-contract.json flips current_subphase to RP00.P01.M05.S07 with status "production_ready" and adds the S07 subphase_closeouts entry as "production_ready" (evidence_root docs/goal-closeout/rp00-p01-m05-s07). The completion_gates now point claude_cross_validation/production_readiness at docs/goal-closeout/rp00-p01-m05-s07, and the validator adds a construction-inspection check requiring inspection.human_approval_reference_relationship_map_verified === true for that closeout. The supplied run shows `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate` exit_code 1 (expected_pending: S07 evidence files not created yet), so no Claude C00 result, Hermes H00 row, or construction-inspection.json exists under that evidence_root. The diff alone cannot satisfy hermes_validation, claude_cross_validation, construction_inspection, or production_readiness gates.
findings.recommendation: Before certifying production_ready, record the actual claude-opus-4-8 effort=max read-only C00 review result (claude-review-result.json), the Hermes H00 evidence row, and construction-inspection.json with human_approval_reference_relationship_map_verified=true under docs/goal-closeout/rp00-p01-m05-s07, then re-run `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate` and confirm exit 0. This is the expected final closeout step; the code, fixture, contract definition, and tests are otherwise complete.
findings.evidence: S07 packet, command evidence, Claude review result, adjudication, and construction inspection are recorded under docs/goal-closeout/rp00-p01-m05-s07; final validation rerun is recorded in command evidence.

findings.title: Divergent blocked_claim reference patterns between S06 ownership routes and S07 relationship map
findings.details: packages/control-plane/src/model.js keeps HUMAN_APPROVAL_CORRECTION_ROUTE_PATTERN from S06 with underscore-only segments ((?:_[a-z0-9]+)*) while S07 adds HUMAN_APPROVAL_BLOCKED_CLAIM_REF_PATTERN allowing dot-or-underscore segments ((?:[._][a-z0-9]+)*). The same conceptual blocked_claim.* reference is therefore accepted under two different canonical shapes across HumanApproval slices.
findings.recommendation: Unify the blocked_claim reference pattern across HumanApproval slices, or explicitly document why relationship-map blocked_claim refs may contain dotted segments while ownership correction routes may not.
findings.evidence: S07 follows the existing HermesGate relationship-map reference pattern for blocked_claim_refs. Required/optional field presence and canonicalization consolidation are explicitly deferred to RP00.P01.M05.S08/S09, where the HumanApproval field registries will decide whether ownership correction routes and relationship references must share one pattern.

findings.evidence: Tabs were normalized to spaces in scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs; rg for tab characters returns no matches and node --check passes.

Formal required/optional field presence is owned by RP00.P01.M05.S08 (Required field registry), so reconcile there.
findings.evidence: validateHumanApprovalReferenceRelationships now coerces omitted blocked_claim_refs, hermes_gate_refs, and claude_review_gate_refs to [] before validation; focused tests and RP00 validator positive cases cover omitted optional arrays.
